generic_rules: Focus repairs on resolving identifier references and type mismatches reported by the validator.
Prioritize fixing existing expressions over adding new architecture.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are defined within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Ensure helper declarations use valid AGREE types: `int`, `real`, or `bool`.
Range checks should use boolean expressions: `eq ok : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value;`
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new AADL architecture to resolve AGREE validation errors.
Avoid using undefined or incorrectly typed identifiers in AGREE expressions.
Do not attempt to use AADL features that are not visible in the current scope.
irrelevant_topics: AADL structural extraction details (component types, implementations, features, connections, properties, and owners)
AGREE contract placement in component types vs. implementations
